task: I had a minor car issue come up and I’m trying to make sure I’d handle it the right way if it turned into an insurance thing. Can you help me make a simple checklist of what to do and what details to save so I’m not scrambling later?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a simple, practical checklist you can keep on your phone or in the car.

## If a minor car issue happens
1. **Stay safe first**
   - Pull over if needed
   - Turn on hazard lights
   - Check for injuries
   - Call 911 if anyone is hurt or traffic is blocked

2. **Don’t admit fault on the spot**
   - Just exchange info and document what happened

3. **Take photos right away**
   - All vehicles from multiple angles
   - License plates
   - Damage close-up and wider shot
   - Road conditions, signs, skid marks, debris
   - Time/date if visible

4. **Exchange information**
   - Name
   - Phone number
   - Insurance company and policy number
   - Driver’s license number
   - Plate number
   - Vehicle make/model/color
   - Witness names and contact info, if any

5. **Write down the basics**
   - Exact location
   - Date and time
   - What happened, in your own words
   - Weather and traffic conditions
   - Police report number, if one is made

6. **If police respond**
   - Get the officer’s name and badge number
   - Ask how to get the report number

7. **Contact your insurance company**
   - Report it as soon as practical
   - Give them the facts only
   - Send photos and notes

## What to save for insurance
Keep these together in one folder or note:
- Photos/video
- Other driver’s info
- Your insurance card
- Police report number
- Witness info
- Repair estimates
- Tow receipt or rental receipt, if any
- A short timeline of what happened

## Handy “don’t forget” notes
- Save everything with the **date**
- Back up photos to cloud or email them to yourself
- Keep a copy of your insurance card in the car
- Keep a spare key and charger handy too, if possible

If you want, I can turn this into a **one-screen phone checklist** or a **printable glovebox version**.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, this works. Simple and actually usable. The part about saving photos, report number, witness info, and repair/tow receipts is the main thing I’d want. I’d probably add: keep a note of who you talked to at insurance and the claim number too, because that’s the stuff I end up needing later when the paperwork starts bouncing around. But overall, solid checklist.
